heading: Definitions
label: 1501 Definitions
notes: Severability, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. Q, title II, § 212(f)134 Stat. 2200Dec. 27, 2020“If any provision of this section [see Short Title of 2020 Amendment note set out under ], an amendment made by this section, or the application of such provision or amendment to any person or circumstance is held to be unconstitutional, the remainder of this section and the amendments made by this section, and the application of the provision or the amendment to any other person or circumstance, shall not be affected.”section 101 of this title
amendments: Severability, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. Q, title II, § 212(f)134 Stat. 2200Dec. 27, 2020“If any provision of this section [see Short Title of 2020 Amendment note set out under ], an amendment made by this section, or the application of such provision or amendment to any person or circumstance is held to be unconstitutional, the remainder of this section and the amendments made by this section, and the application of the provision or the amendment to any other person or circumstance, shall not be affected.”section 101 of this title

children.text: the term “claimant” means the real party in interest that commences a proceeding before the Copyright Claims Board under section 1506(e), pursuant to a permissible claim of infringement brought under section 1504(c)(1), noninfringement brought under section 1504(c)(2), or misrepresentation brought under section 1504(c)(3);

children.label: A
children.text: asserts a permissible counterclaim under section 1504(c)(4) against the claimant in the proceeding; and

children.label: B
children.text: is the real party in interest with respect to the counterclaim described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: B
children.text: includes the attorney of a party, as applicable; and

the term “respondent” means any person against whom a proceeding is brought before the Copyright Claims Board under section 1506(e), pursuant to a permissible claim of infringement brought under section 1504(c)(1), noninfringement brought under section 1504(c)(2), or misrepresentation brought under section 1504(c)(3).
